extraction.one_line_summary: CARUSO Damiano soloed from the breakaway on the Velifique MTF, holding off ROGLIC Primoz by 1:05 while BERNAL Egan cracked badly.
tactical_patterns: CARUSO extended breakaway gap to 5:15 before final climb; peloton tempo control error allowed stage-hunting domestique freedom.
Ineos raised pace on Velifique lower slopes after disliking Jumbo tempo, signaling they wanted selection but lacked firepower to control.
ROGLIC sprinted for second place finish specifically to collect time bonuses, revealing GC tactical awareness even after stage lost.
YATES Adam identified as most aggressive with least to lose, suggesting domestique-turned-leader willing to gamble on backfiring attacks.

rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: cracked badly on Velifique MTF, losing 1:11 to ROGLIC including bonuses, forcing Ineos tactical rethink mid-race.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: controlled MOSS acceleration then out-sprinted him for time bonuses, showing superior explosive finishing power at altitude.
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: timed solo bridge perfectly to dominate breakaway, held 1:05 gap despite bahrain co-leader LANDA absent, unexpected GC threat.
rider_signals.MOSS Enrique: positive: consolidated second overall by matching ROGLIC accelerations, moved to nearly 2:00 behind after stage shakeup.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: repeatedly aggressive but Ineos lacked collective strength to capitalize, best-positioned for high-risk attacks going forward.
rider_signals.HAIG Jack: positive: best of rest for Bahrain behind CARUSO, fourth on stage without team support, emerging as unexpected GC option.
